title: Remove or hide glossary page
description: glossary.html is a placeholder. Remove it from the nav and either delete the file or hide it until there is real content.
close_reason: Removed glossary.html and glossary nav link from all pages.

title: compile_essay tests write to project tree instead of tmp dir
Add table of contents to essay pages
description: Add a top-of-page table of contents to essay pages, inferred from H2 headings via JavaScript. Should be collapsible/minimal, matching the site's monospace aesthetic. Positioned below the essay header, above the essay body. Style with IBM Plex Mono to match nav elements.

title: Feed wave 3: Predrag Gruevski (Mastodon)
description:
Add Predrag Gruevski's Mastodon feed (hachyderm.io/@predrag.rss) to _data/feeds.json.

Predrag is the author of cargo-semver-checks (automated semver verification for Rust crates, being merged into cargo itself) and Trustfall (a query engine that treats any data source as queryable — used under the hood for semver linting). Derived from Nesbitt's Mastodon network — directly at the intersection of provenance verification and dependency management.

Note: no blog RSS found, only Mastodon feed. Mastodon posts tend to be shorter/noisier than blog feeds — may want to add filter_exclude terms to keep signal high. Test feed quality before committing. Consider revisiting if he starts a blog with proper RSS.

title: Tufte-style sidenotes for essays
description: Inline marginalia in the right margin alongside referenced text, instead of footnotes at page bottom. Shift essay body left to make room for sidenote column while keeping braille sidebars. CSS-only implementation, falls back to expandable inline notes on mobile. Pandoc template outputs sidenote markup from footnote syntax.

title: Multi-version essay sync: compile all snapshots in a lineage
description:
Rework the publish pipeline to sync an entire knots lineage rather than a single snapshot.

Current state: codex-ykf added a single-shot workflow_dispatch that fetches one publish ref and compiles it.

New behavior:
- git ls-remote to list all refs/knots/publish/{lineage}/* snapshots
- Skip snapshots already compiled (immutable — compile once)
- Compile each new snapshot to /essays/{id}/{version}/
- Latest (by manifest published_at) also served at /essays/{id}/
- Registry (_data/essays.json) tracks all versions per essay, not just current
- Use manifest published_at (not frontmatter date) for display dates on site

Date semantics (tentative):
- frontmatter date = authoring date, displayed on the essay page itself
- manifest published_at = per-version publish timestamp, used in version list and registry

Version UI:
- Essay pages need an in-page version picker/history (not just bare URLs)
- Design TBD: dropdown, collapsible section, sidebar list, etc.
- Each version is a full compiled page at /essays/{id}/{version}/
- Latest version also served at /essays/{id}/

Knots model (from knots agent):
- Snapshot name = version identifier (e.g. v1.0)
- manifest.json has published_at timestamp for ordering
- manifest.json has parent field linking to previous snapshot
- Snapshots are immutable once published
- No 'latest' pointer — use published_at to determine latest

Touches: workflow, compile script, update_registry, registry schema, essay template.

title: Sentence-level permalinks from knots IDs
description: Render knots sentence IDs (from .knots.md) as invisible anchor elements in essay HTML. Enables linking to specific sentences for citation at sub-paragraph granularity. IDs like [3f8a:h1] become anchor targets. Requires consuming .knots.md in addition to .md from publish artifacts.

title: Add test suite for multi-version essay pipeline
Build essay preprocessor
description: Create build/preprocess.py (stdlib only). Transforms paper_v2.md into Pandoc-ready markdown: sentence IDs ([f8fb] -> hidden span anchors), section markers (`[pa]` -> section-break divs), YAML frontmatter merging from paper.yaml. Must handle all 132 sentence IDs and 7 section markers in the source essay.
close_reason: Not needed — source repo renderer handles sentence IDs and section markers

title: Create archive page for historical essays
description: Create an archive page listing prefatory/historical works from the site's early days: 'decadence, extinction, whites: allegory' (Google Drive PDF), 'Autologist Fragments' (Google Doc), and the embedded essay (Google Doc iframe). Include cypherdude.jpeg as bottom art (resources/cypherdude.jpeg, CSS already in site.css as .bottom-art-img). Link from nav. These are external links, not knots-managed publications.
close_reason: Archive page created with two historical essay links and cypherdude bottom art. Nav links added to all pages.

title: Feed wave 2: samwho.dev, Lars Wirzenius
description:
Add two more RSS feeds to _data/feeds.json and rebuild.

1. Sam Rose / samwho.dev (samwho.dev/rss.xml) — Visual essays about software internals: load balancing, memory allocation, scheduling. High production quality, technical depth. Derived from Nesbitt's Mastodon network — represents the 'making systems legible' thread, adjacent to source engineering's concern with making source artifacts transparent and verifiable.

2. Lars Wirzenius / blog.liw.fi (blog.liw.fi/index.atom) — Works full-time on Radicle (P2P code collaboration). Long history in Debian, backup systems, infrastructure. Derived from Nesbitt's network — Radicle is building decentralized code hosting, directly relevant to source provenance and forge-independent collaboration.

Both feeds confirmed working. Add to feeds.json, rebuild, verify.

title: Configure deploy key for source_engineering repo
description: Generate ed25519 deploy key. Add public key as read-only deploy key on codex-journal/source_engineering. Add private key as SOURCE_ENGINEERING_DEPLOY_KEY secret on codex-journal/codex-journal.github.io. The Action fetches refs/knots/publish/ from source_engineering — read-only access to publish refs only, no access to private source branches needed. Manual setup in GitHub UI.
close_reason: Deploy key configured manually in GitHub UI

title: Add nav links to essay pages
description: Essay pages (essays/*/index.html) only have Glossary in nav. Need to add Feeds and Archive links to match the rest of the site. Currently generated by Pandoc template — need to update the template or post-process.
close_reason: Created essays.html page with template + build integration. Updated nav site-wide (Essays, Glossary, Feeds, Archive) on all pages: index-template, feeds-template, glossary, archive, and essay pages.

title: Add GitHub activity feed to index page
description: Display recent GitHub activity on the index page. Fetch via RSS/Atom feed at build time (not client-side JS) and bake into static HTML. build_index.py pulls the feed, renders a recent activity section below the essay listing. Rebuilt on cron schedule (daily) or on push via GitHub Actions. Same build step that regenerates the index from the essay registry.
close_reason: Implemented multi-feed system: _data/feeds.json registry, build_index.py fetches Atom+RSS, index has collapsible 5-item preview, dedicated /feeds.html with per-group sections (Codex Journal, Source Engineering). Dynamic feed titles from XML.

title: Clean up index page placeholder
description: The live index.html shows [Title] and [Author] placeholders in the essay listing. Either generate the listing from _data/essays.json via build_index.py, or hide/remove the placeholder entry until the first essay is actually published.

title: Build essay registry and index generator
description: Create _data/essays.json registry format, build/update_registry.py (stamps version/date/ref into registry), and build/build_index.py (generates index.html landing page from registry listing published essays with title, version, date, link).

title: Atom feed for published essays
description: Produce an Atom/RSS feed of published essays so readers can subscribe. Generated at build time by build_index.py or a separate script. Include title, author, date, version, abstract, and link for each essay entry.

title: Essay provenance display
description: Show BLAKE3 hashes, version chain, and state_root from knots manifest.json on each essay page. Readers can independently verify document integrity. Display snapshot lineage (parent versions) and publish metadata. Fits the cyphernetics ethos — cryptographic verification of published work.

title: Feed: Simon Willison
description:
Add Simon Willison's Atom feed (simonwillison.net/atom/everything/) to _data/feeds.json.

Simon Willison — creator of Datasette, Django co-creator. Prolific writer on AI tooling, LLMs, agentic patterns, data exploration. High signal, high volume. Not directly source engineering — more adjacent through the tooling/infrastructure angle. May need filter_exclude to manage volume since his 'everything' feed includes entries, links, quotes, notes, and guides.

Feed URL confirmed working. Filed as wave 1 priority but grouping TBD — doesn't fit neatly into the Nesbitt-derived source engineering cluster. May warrant its own feed group or a broader 'Tools & Infrastructure' group. Evaluate fit after adding.

title: Dispatches — short-form notes and links
description: A section for shorter pieces: links with commentary, notes, observations. Less ceremony than full essays. Simple markdown-to-HTML build, own listing page and feed entries. Framed as transmissions/dispatches.

title: Feed wave 1: Filippo Valsorda, Greg Wilson
description:
Add two high-signal RSS feeds to _data/feeds.json and rebuild.

1. Filippo Valsorda (words.filippo.io/rss/) — Cryptographic and security engineering. Founding and lead iOS engineer at Signal. Writes about Go cryptography, certificate transparency, key management. Derived from Andrew Nesbitt's Mastodon network (mastodon.social/@andrewnez following list) — his bio reads 'Cryptographic & Security Engineering' and he's adjacent to the provenance/integrity thread that runs through source engineering.

2. Greg Wilson (third-bit.com/atom.xml) — Co-founder of Software Carpentry, ran 'It Will Never Work in Theory' (bridging CS research and practice). Writes about research software, teaching, and the sociology of programming. Derived from Nesbitt's network — represents the metascience/research-software wing of the source engineering cluster.

Both feeds confirmed working. Add to feeds.json under a new group (e.g. 'Adjacent'), run build_index.py, verify on feeds page.

title: Create Pandoc template and build config
description: Create build/essay-template.html (Pandoc HTML template wrapping essay in site nav, header, body, references, footer) and build/essay-defaults.yaml (CSL style, link-citations, reference-section-title). Download and commit chicago-author-date.csl. Input is rendered .md from knots publish ref (refs/knots/publish/{lineage}/{snapshot_name}/), not raw source. Test full local Pandoc pipeline with a publish artifact.

title: Set up GitHub Action for essay publishing
description: Create .github/workflows/publish-essay.yml with workflow_dispatch trigger. Inputs: essay_id, lineage, snapshot_name. Steps: fetch refs/knots/publish/{lineage}/{snapshot_name}/ from source_engineering repo via deploy key, read manifest.json for metadata (state_root, parent, artifact hashes), run Pandoc with citeproc on the .md artifact, update _data/essays.json registry, regenerate index, commit compiled output. No private source checkout needed — only fetches immutable publish artifacts.

title: Colophon page
description: Describe the journal's toolchain and publishing philosophy. Knots for structured source versioning, beads for issue tracking, Pandoc for rendering, braille art from CAPTCHAs, BLAKE3 integrity verification. The 'how this journal is made' page.
